tools.name: ImageDescription
tools.description: A useful tool that returns a brief description of the input image.

tools.name: RegionAttributeDescription
tools.description: Describe the attribute of a region of the input image.

inputs.name: bbox
inputs.description: The bbox coordinate in the format of `(x1, y1, x2, y2)`

dialogs.content: You are given a histology figure, which staining is done?

dialogs.thought: Orient to modality and staining patterns across panels (H&E vs IHC markers).

content.content: A multi-panel histology figure with four columns (KM12L4, pcDNA3, Ephrin-B2 S2, Ephrin-B2 S2 pool) and three rows labeled H&E, CD31, and PCNA. Top row shows pink/purple H&E tumor tissue; middle and bottom rows show brown immunohistochemical staining patterns consistent with marker expression.

arguments.attribute: staining appearance and what it indicates

dialogs.thought: Verify that the lower row demonstrates typical IHC chromogen signal localizing antigen within cells/tissue.

content.content: The region shows numerous cells with brown nuclear/cytoplasmic chromogen staining against a lighter background, consistent with immunohistochemical positivity indicating localization of the targeted antigen within the tissue section.

dialogs.content: Immunohistochemical staining is a technique used to visualize and identify specific proteins or antigens within tissue samples. It involves the use of antibodies that bind to the target protein or antigen, followed by a detection system that produces a visible signal, such as a colored precipitate or a fluorescent signal. This method allows researchers and pathologists to study the distribution, localization, and expression levels of specific proteins within cells and tissues, which can provide valuable information about the function, structure, and potential abnormalities in the sample.
